name: Log Stream
description: Real-time log streaming display with filtering, search, and auto-scroll behavior.

filtered.description: Log stream with level filter controls

grouped.description: Logs grouped by source or timestamp

visual_brief: Terminal-style scrolling log display on a dark background with monospace text. Each log entry is a single line showing a timestamp in muted color, a log level badge (INFO in blue, WARN in yellow, ERROR in red, DEBUG in gray), and the message text. New entries append at the bottom with auto-scroll behavior. A controls header contains a search input, level filter toggles, and a pause/resume button. The filtered preset dims non-matching entries. The grouped preset collapses repeated messages with a count badge. A small stats bar shows entries/second and total count.

responsive.mobile: Full-width log view. Controls collapse into a top bar with a filter toggle button. Log entries use smaller font. Search is a full-width overlay.
responsive.tablet: Standard layout with controls visible. Comfortable font size for log entries.
responsive.desktop: Full log stream with controls bar, search, and stats visible. Wide view accommodates long log messages without wrapping.

layout_hints.terminal_frame: The pattern owns the terminal-like frame, controls header, and scrolling stream region. Parent layouts should not wrap it in another faux-terminal shell.
layout_hints.signal_hierarchy: Lead with the live stream, then controls, then secondary counters. Stats should support stream interpretation, not compete with the entries.
layout_hints.line_density: Entries should align in a stable rhythm with timestamp, level, source, and message fields that scan quickly during live updates.

motion.micro: New entries may fade or slide in subtly at the bottom, and paused state indicators may brighten clearly when active.
motion.transitions: Auto-scroll should feel steady and predictable. Filter changes should dim or reveal entries without causing the whole stream frame to jump.

keyboard: Tab through stream controls, search, and any copy/export actions before entering the log viewport.
If the log viewport itself is focusable, arrow keys and Page Up/Page Down should preserve readable navigation through entries.
announcements: New entries, pause state, and active filters should be exposed in a way that does not overwhelm assistive technology with every visual update.
